quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 15 Quiz 9
quiz.subtitle: 15.9: Applications of Parametric Equations (Projectile Motion)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Modeling motion over time using parametric equations

questions.question: A projectile is launched at $80$ ft/s at a $30^{\circ}$ angle from ground level. Find its horizontal position after $2$ seconds. Use $\cos 30^{\circ} \approx 0.866$.
A projectile is launched at $80$ ft/s at a $30^{\circ}$ angle from ground level. Find its height after $2$ seconds. Use $\sin 30^{\circ} = 0.5$, $g = 32$ ft/s².
A ball is launched from ground level at $96$ ft/s at a $30^{\circ}$ angle. How long is it in the air? Use $\sin 30^{\circ} = 0.5$.
A ball is launched vertically ($\theta = 90^{\circ}$) at $64$ ft/s from ground level. Find its maximum height. Use $\sin 90^{\circ} = 1$.
A projectile is launched at $80$ ft/s at $30^{\circ}$ from ground level. Find the horizontal range. Use $\sin 30^{\circ}=0.5$, $\cos 30^{\circ}\approx0.866$.
questions.explanation: Time of flight: $t = 2.5$ s. Range: $x = 80(0.866)(2.5) = 173.2$ ft.

questions.question: A projectile launched at $45^{\circ}$ travels $400$ ft horizontally (ground level). Find the initial velocity. Use $\sin 45^{\circ}=\cos 45^{\circ}\approx0.7071$, $g=32$.
A projectile is launched at $60$ ft/s at $30^{\circ}$ from a $20$ ft platform. Find its height when it has traveled $40$ ft horizontally. Use $\cos 30^{\circ}\approx0.866$, $\sin 30^{\circ}=0.5$.
A projectile is launched at $128$ ft/s at $60^{\circ}$. Find the time to reach maximum height. Use $\sin 60^{\circ} \approx 0.866$.
A projectile launched at $100$ ft/s travels $200$ ft (ground level). Find the launch angle(s). Use $g = 32$.
A projectile is launched at $80$ ft/s at $45^{\circ}$ from a $50$ ft cliff. Find the maximum height above ground. Use $\sin 45^{\circ} \approx 0.7071$.

scoreMessages.90: Rocket Scientist! You've mastered projectile motion — horizontal and vertical position, time of flight, maximum height, range, initial velocity from range, height at a given distance, time to peak, launch angles for a given range, and launches from platforms.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Key reminders: (1) $x = (v_0 \cos \theta)t$ — horizontal motion, (2) $y = -16t^2 + (v_0 \sin \theta)t + h_0$ — vertical motion, (3) Time of flight: set $y=0$ and solve, (4) Max height: vertex at $t = \frac{v_0 \sin \theta}{g}$, (5) Range: $R = \frac{v_0^2 \sin(2\theta)}{g}$.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Finding time from one equation and plugging into the other, (2) Using the vertex formula for max height, (3) Solving for $v_0$ or $\theta$ from the range formula, (4) Including the initial height $h_0$ when launching from above ground.
scoreMessages.0: Review projectile motion: (1) Horizontal: $x = (v_0 \cos \theta)t$ — constant velocity, (2) Vertical: $y = -\frac{1}{2}gt^2 + (v_0 \sin \theta)t + h_0$ — affected by gravity, (3) Time of flight: set $y=0$, factor out $t$, solve, (4) Range: $R = \frac{v_0^2 \sin(2\theta)}{g}$ for ground-level launches, (5) Maximum height occurs when vertical velocity = 0, at $t = \frac{v_0 \sin \theta}{g}$.
